text: Mars is a planet purely of robots (for now) https://t.co/wULW6sCT5l
summary: Mars is a planet purely of robots (for now)
ai_summary: Elon Musk comments on Mars colonization strategy, noting that initial Mars settlement will rely entirely on robots rather than human presence. This reflects SpaceX's engineering approach to planetary exploration—prioritizing autonomous systems and robotics development as foundational infrastructure before human missions, demonstrating a phased technical strategy for one of humanity's most ambitious engineering challenges.

parent.text: TERAFAB: On the Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) Q1 2026 earnings call, Chairman & CEO C.C. Wei said the following in regards to the Tesla/SpaceX Terafab Project. "Well, actually, both Intel and Tesla, they are TSMC’s customer. Again, they are our competitors, and we
parent.name: S.E. Robinson, Jr.
parent.embed_html:
TERAFAB: On the Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) Q1 2026 earnings call, Chairman & CEO C.C. Wei said the following in regards to the Tesla/SpaceX Terafab Project.

"Well, actually, both Intel and Tesla, they are TSMC’s customer. Again, they are our competitors, and we… pic.twitter.com/t6VorfFhSh

— S.E. Robinson, Jr. (@SERobinsonJr) April 16, 2026

ai_summary: TSMC's Q1 2026 earnings call revealed discussion of the Tesla/SpaceX Terafab Project, with CEO C.C. Wei addressing the competitive relationship between TSMC's customers (Intel and Tesla). Elon clarifies that SpaceX/Tesla will remain major TSMC customers rather than true competitors, indicating Tesla/SpaceX's semiconductor manufacturing ambitions through Terafab are complementary to rather than directly competitive with TSMC's core business. This reveals strategic positioning around in-house chip production capability while maintaining critical supplier relationships.
embed_html:
SpaceX/Tesla will be always be major customers of TSMC and not competitors in the normal sense of the word

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 18, 2026

text: @SERobinsonJr TSMC just can’t make the staggeringly large number of chips needed! If they could, we would not need to do this.
summary: @SERobinsonJr TSMC just can’t make the staggeringly large number of chips needed! If they could, we would not need to do this.

parent.text: @SERobinsonJr SpaceX/Tesla will be always be major customers of TSMC and not competitors in the normal sense of the word
parent.name: Elon Musk
parent.embed_html:
SpaceX/Tesla will be always be major customers of TSMC and not competitors in the normal sense of the word

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 18, 2026

ai_summary: TSMC's CEO acknowledged that Tesla/SpaceX are both customers and competitors, but Elon clarifies that SpaceX/Tesla are not competitors in the traditional sense—rather, TSMC's production capacity constraints are forcing Tesla and SpaceX to build their own semiconductor manufacturing capability (Terafab) to meet their massive chip demands that TSMC alone cannot fulfill. This reveals a critical business insight: when a single supplier cannot scale to meet your production needs, vertical integration into that supply chain becomes a strategic necessity rather than optional competition.
embed_html:
TSMC just can’t make the staggeringly large number of chips needed! If they could, we would not need to do this.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 18, 2026

summary: @mark_k @xai This is still 0.5T, but a more recent training checkpoint. 1T model is ~5 days away from finishing initial training. Will be a major step change improvement in coding, long context and skills. The SpaceXAI model factory is finally working. Should be an improved base model

parent.text: Grok 4.3 (beta) has been released by @xai 🔥🔥 The new version of Grok is currently available to the highest tier plan SuperGrok Heavy. With 1T params Grok 4.3 is twice as big as Grok 4.20, and was also trained much longer. It should provide a decent jump in performance.
parent.name: Mark Kretschmann
parent.embed_html:
Grok 4.3 (beta) has been released by @xai 🔥🔥

The new version of Grok is currently available to the highest tier plan SuperGrok Heavy.

With 1T params Grok 4.3 is twice as big as Grok 4.20, and was also trained much longer. It should provide a decent jump in performance. pic.twitter.com/EygczR8eaX

— Mark Kretschmann (@mark_k) April 17, 2026

ai_summary: xAI released Grok 4.3 beta, but Elon clarified it remains a 0.5T parameter model (not 1T as initially claimed) while a full 1T model is ~5 days away from completing initial training. This reflects SpaceX's AI infrastructure ('SpaceXAI model factory') enabling rapid iteration, with the larger model expected to deliver significant improvements in coding, long context handling, and overall capabilities—demonstrating how Musk's companies are scaling AI training compute and execution velocity.
embed_html:
This is still 0.5T, but a more recent training checkpoint.

1T model is ~5 days away from finishing initial training. Will be a major step change improvement in coding, long context and skills.

The SpaceXAI model factory is finally working. Should be an improved base model…

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 18, 2026
